task.description: E-commerce API: look up user, last order, product details, shipping status, and summarize
task.prompt: Using the available tools, look up user with ID 42, find their most recent order, get the product details for the item in that order, and check the shipping status. Then print a summary that includes the user's name, order ID, product name, product price, and shipping status.

tools.name: get_user
tools.description: Fetch user profile by ID

tools.name: list_orders
tools.description: List orders for a user, returns most recent first

tools.name: get_order
tools.description: Get order details by order ID

tools.name: get_product
tools.description: Fetch product details by product ID

tools.name: get_shipping_status
tools.description: Get shipping tracking info for an order

tools.name: list_products
tools.description: List all products with optional category filter

tools.name: search_products
tools.description: Search products by keyword

tools.name: get_inventory
tools.description: Check inventory levels for a product

tools.name: get_reviews
tools.description: Get product reviews

tools.name: create_return
tools.description: Initiate a product return

tools.name: apply_coupon
tools.description: Apply a coupon code to an order
tools.mock: {"error":"coupon cannot be applied to shipped orders"}

tools.name: get_payment
tools.description: Get payment details for an order

tools.name: list_addresses
tools.description: List saved addresses for a user

tools.name: get_wishlist
tools.description: Get user's wishlist

tools.name: get_cart
tools.description: Get user's current shopping cart

tools.name: list_categories
tools.description: List all product categories

tools.name: get_promotions
tools.description: Get active promotions

tools.name: get_loyalty_points
tools.description: Get loyalty points balance for a user

content.text: Using the available tools, look up user with ID 42, find their most recent order, get the product details for the item in that order, and check the shipping status. Then print a summary that includes the user's name, order ID, product name, product price, and shipping status.

input.commands:
set -e
user=$(get_user --user_id 42)
order=$(list_orders --limit 1 --user_id 42)
order_id=$(echo "$order" | jq -r '.[0].order_id')
order_details=$(get_order --order_id "$order_id")
product_id=$(echo "$order_details" | jq -r '.items[0].product_id')
product=$(get_product --product_id "$product_id")
shipping=$(get_shipping_status --order_id "$order_id")
user_name=$(echo "$user" | jq -r '.name')
product_name=$(echo "$product" | jq -r '.name')
product_price=$(echo "$product" | jq -r '.price')
shipping_status=$(echo "$shipping" | jq -r '.status')
printf 'User: %s\nOrder ID: %s\nProduct: %s\nPrice: %s\nShipping Status: %s\n' "$user_name" "$order_id" "$product_name" "$product_price" "$shipping_status"

content.text:
User: Jane Doe  
Order ID: ORD-1001  
Product: Wireless Headphones  
Price: 39.99  
Shipping Status: In Transit

input.commands:
set -e
user=$(get_user --user_id 42)
order=$(list_orders --limit 1 --user_id 42)
order_id=$(echo "$order" | jq -r '.[0].order_id')
order_details=$(get_order --order_id "$order_id")
product_id=$(echo "$order_details" | jq -r '.items[0].product_id')
product=$(get_product --product_id "$product_id")
shipping=$(get_shipping_status --order_id "$order_id")
user_name=$(echo "$user" | jq -r '.name')
product_name=$(echo "$product" | jq -r '.name')
product_price=$(echo "$product" | jq -r '.price')
shipping_status=$(echo "$shipping" | jq -r '.status')
printf 'User: %s\nOrder ID: %s\nProduct: %s\nPrice: %s\nShipping Status: %s\n' "$user_name" "$order_id" "$product_name" "$product_price" "$shipping_status"

task.description: CRM system: look up customer, get support tickets, check subscription, generate summary report
task.prompt: Using the available tools, look up customer 'Acme Corp' by name, retrieve their open support tickets, check their current subscription status, and get their account manager info. Print a summary report that includes: company name, account manager, subscription plan, subscription status, number of open tickets, and the subject of each open ticket.

tools.name: search_customers
tools.description: Search customers by name or domain

tools.name: get_customer
tools.description: Get customer details by ID

tools.name: list_tickets
tools.description: List support tickets for a customer

tools.name: get_ticket
tools.description: Get a single support ticket by ID

tools.name: get_subscription
tools.description: Get subscription details for a customer

tools.name: get_employee
tools.description: Get employee / account manager info

tools.name: get_interactions
tools.description: Get recent interactions/notes for a customer

tools.name: get_invoices
tools.description: List invoices for a customer

tools.name: get_usage_metrics
tools.description: Get product usage metrics for a customer

tools.name: get_health_score
tools.description: Get computed health score for a customer

tools.description: List contacts at a customer organization

tools.name: create_note
tools.description: Add a note to a customer record

tools.description: Create a follow-up task for the account team

content.text: Using the available tools, look up customer 'Acme Corp' by name, retrieve their open support tickets, check their current subscription status, and get their account manager info. Print a summary report that includes: company name, account manager, subscription plan, subscription status, number of open tickets, and the subject of each open ticket.

input.commands:
set -e
customer=$(search_customers --query 'Acme Corp' | jq '.[0]')
customer_id=$(echo "$customer" | jq -r '.id')
company_name=$(echo "$customer" | jq -r '.name')
open_tickets=$(list_tickets --customer_id "$customer_id" --status open)
subscription=$(get_subscription --customer_id "$customer_id")
am_id=$(echo "$customer" | jq -r '.account_manager_id')
if [ "$am_id" = "null" ] || [ -z "$am_id" ]; then
  am_id=$(get_customer --customer_id "$customer_id" | jq -r '.account_manager_id')
fi
account_manager=$(get_employee --employee_id "$am_id")
plan=$(echo "$subscription" | jq -r '.plan')
sub_status=$(echo "$subscription" | jq -r '.status')
am_name=$(echo "$account_manager" | jq -r '.name')
ticket_count=$(echo "$open_tickets" | jq 'length')
echo "Company Name: $company_name"
echo "Account Manager: $am_name"
echo "Subscription Plan: $plan"
echo "Subscription Status: $sub_status"
echo "Open Tickets: $ticket_count"
echo "Ticket Subjects:"
echo "$open_tickets" | jq -r '.[] | "- " + .subject'

task.description: Analytics platform: get daily metrics, compare with previous day, identify anomalies
task.prompt: Using the available tools, retrieve the analytics metrics for 2025-12-15 and compare them with the previous day 2025-12-14. Identify any anomalies (metrics that changed by more than 20%). Print a report showing: each metric name, its value on both days, the percentage change, and flag any anomalies. Focus on page_views, unique_visitors, bounce_rate, avg_session_duration, and conversion_rate.

content.text: Using the available tools, retrieve the analytics metrics for 2025-12-15 and compare them with the previous day 2025-12-14. Identify any anomalies (metrics that changed by more than 20%). Print a report showing: each metric name, its value on both days, the percentage change, and flag any anomalies. Focus on page_views, unique_visitors, bounce_rate, avg_session_duration, and conversion_rate.

Analytics comparison report: 2025-12-15 vs 2025-12-14

| Metric | 2025-12-14 | 2025-12-15 | % Change | Anomaly |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| page_views | 52,100 | 45,200 | -13.24% | No |
| unique_visitors | 14,200 | 12,800 | -9.86% | No |
| bounce_rate | 0.31 | 0.34 | +9.68% | No |
| avg_session_duration | 260 | 245 | -5.77% | No |
| conversion_rate | 0.038 | 0.042 | +10.53% | No |

Anomaly rule used: flagged if absolute percentage change > 20%.

Summary:
- No anomalies detected among the requested metrics.
- Largest decrease: page_views at -13.24%.
- Largest increase: conversion_rate at +10.53%.

task.description: DevOps monitoring: check service health, recent deployments, error rates, determine rollback need
task.prompt: Using the available tools, check the current health of the 'payment-service', look at its recent deployments, check its error rate, and review recent alerts. Determine whether the latest deployment should be rolled back. Print a status report including: service health status, latest deployment version and timestamp, current error rate, whether the error rate is above the 1% threshold, and your rollback recommendation with reasoning.

content.text: Using the available tools, check the current health of the 'payment-service', look at its recent deployments, check its error rate, and review recent alerts. Determine whether the latest deployment should be rolled back. Print a status report including: service health status, latest deployment version and timestamp, current error rate, whether the error rate is above the 1% threshold, and your rollback recommendation with reasoning.

Status Report: payment-service

- Service health status: degraded
  - Healthy instances: 3/5
  - Uptime: 97.2%
  - Last check: 2025-12-15T14:30:00Z

- Latest deployment:
  - Version: v2.4.1
  - Deploy ID: DEP-450
  - Timestamp: 2025-12-15T12:00:00Z

- Current error rate:
  - Error rate: 3.2%
  - Errors/requests: 156/4875
  - Above 1% threshold: yes

- Recent open alerts:
  - Critical: Error rate exceeded 3% threshold (triggered 2025-12-15T12:45:00Z)
  - Warning: 2 instances unhealthy (triggered 2025-12-15T12:50:00Z)

Rollback recommendation: Yes, recommend rollback of the latest deployment.

Reasoning:
- The service is currently degraded, with only 3 of 5 instances healthy.
- The current error rate is 3.2%, which is well above the 1% threshold.
- A critical alert for elevated error rate triggered shortly after the latest deployment at 2025-12-15T12:00:00Z.
- There is also an active warning indicating unhealthy instances.
- Given the timing and severity of the symptoms after the latest deployment, the latest deployment is a strong candidate for causing the regression, so rollback is warranted.
